quick_facts.label: Central-government designation
quick_facts.value: In CDSCO usage, ADC(I) means Assistant Drugs Controller (India).

quick_facts.label: Central service classification
quick_facts.value: Under the 2024 CDSCO Medical Device Vertical recruitment rules, Assistant Drugs Controller (India) is a General Central Service, Group A, Gazetted, Non-Ministerial post.

quick_facts.label: Illustrative central recruitment mix
quick_facts.value: For the Medical Device Vertical, the 2024 rules prescribe 50% direct recruitment and 50% promotion, with deputation (including short-term contract) if promotion cannot fill vacancies.

quick_facts.label: Drug Inspector feeder route
quick_facts.value: In that Medical Device Vertical cadre, Drugs Inspectors in Pay Level 8 may be promoted to ADC(I) after eight years of regular service and the prescribed mandatory training.

quick_facts.label: Illustrative central pay context
quick_facts.value: The 2024 Medical Device Vertical rules place ADC(I) at Pay Level 11, ₹67,700–₹2,08,700 in the pay matrix. This is a dated basic-pay-scale reference; actual monthly remuneration varies with applicable allowances, posting, deductions, and later pay-rule changes.

quick_facts.label: Typical central postings
quick_facts.value: CDSCO identifies ADC(I) officers in zonal and port offices, including offices serving customs/import-export functions.

sections.heading: What an Assistant Drugs Controller does
paragraphs: The role is supervisory and regulatory rather than solely inspection-based. In a CDSCO sub-zonal disclosure, an ADC(I) is described as ensuring uniform implementation of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 with the State Licensing Authority; monitoring Drugs Inspectors and other staff; overseeing court cases and statutory reports; allocating and reviewing licence/certificate applications; and arranging or conducting manufacturing-site inspections before regulatory action.

At CDSCO port offices, the work can focus on import and export control: supervising scrutiny of Bills of Entry, checking regulatory compliance of drugs, medical devices, IVDs and cosmetics, arranging risk-based physical verification, issuing or supporting no-objection clearances, coordinating with Customs, and preventing release of consignments not permitted by the competent authority. The exact mix depends on the office and jurisdiction.
bullets: Supervise inspection, application-review, enforcement, reporting, and case-management work.
Apply the Drugs and Cosmetics Act and associated rules within the authority delegated to the office.
Coordinate with state licensing authorities, Customs, laboratories, applicants, and other investigating agencies where relevant.

sections.heading: Entry routes and eligibility context
paragraphs: Routes are cadre- and notification-specific. The central CDSCO Medical Device Vertical rules notified in 2024 provide a useful official example: direct recruits may qualify with a relevant master's degree plus four years' experience, or a relevant bachelor's degree plus six years' experience, in manufacturing, testing, regulation, or design of medical devices. The listed disciplines include pharmacy/pharmaceutical sciences, life sciences, chemistry, microbiology, medicine with clinical pharmacology, and several engineering fields.

The same rules establish an internal route from Drugs Inspector (Medical Devices): eight years of regular service in Level 8 plus prescribed mandatory training. They also permit deputation from qualifying government, public-sector, university, research, statutory, or autonomous-organisation officers. Candidates should treat these as rules for that specific central vertical—not as a universal eligibility rule for every state Assistant Drugs Controller post—and use the relevant recruitment notification as controlling.
bullets: Build an eligible academic foundation in the discipline specified in the target cadre's notification.
Gain documented experience in regulated manufacturing, testing, inspection, regulatory review, or the relevant technical domain.
For the internal central Medical Device Vertical route, progress through the Drugs Inspector cadre and complete required service and training.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The official duties and recruitment rules point to a blend of technical-regulatory and supervisory capability. Candidates need enough scientific and product knowledge to evaluate manufacturing, testing, quality, safety, and compliance questions, together with working command of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act framework and the regulations applicable to the office's portfolio.

The role also calls for inspection planning and review, evidence-based documentation, clear regulatory correspondence, report writing, case and file management, and coordination with field inspectors and external agencies. For port postings, practical familiarity with import/export documentation and Customs coordination is particularly relevant.
bullets: Technical evaluation of drug, cosmetic, medical-device, or laboratory/manufacturing records, according to the assigned cadre.
Inspection oversight, risk-based verification, documentation, and defensible reporting.

sections.heading: Where the work happens
paragraphs: Assistant Drugs Controller posts can be based in central CDSCO zonal, sub-zonal, and port offices. CDSCO's official directory lists ADC(I) positions alongside Deputy Drugs Controllers in regional/zonal offices, while its port-office directory lists ADC(I) offices at locations such as Delhi, Kolkata, Mumbai, and Chennai.

Work therefore combines office-based regulatory review and administration with field-facing activity: inspections of applicant facilities, oversight of inspectorate work, meetings with state authorities or Customs, verification of consignments, and handling of reports, court matters, RTI matters, or stakeholder communications as assigned.
bullets: Central zonal and sub-zonal regulatory offices.
Airport and seaport offices dealing with regulated imports and exports.
State drug-control administrations, where the title, hierarchy, recruitment rules, and delegated work may differ.

sections.heading: Progression from Drug Inspector
paragraphs: For the CDSCO Medical Device Vertical, the formal progression route is Drugs Inspector (Medical Devices) to Assistant Drugs Controller (India), subject to eight years of regular service in the feeder grade and mandatory training. The next higher central position is Deputy Drugs Controller (India): the 2025 recruitment rules for that post specify promotion from ADC(I) in Level 11 after five years' regular service and prescribed training.

This gives the node's parent-child relationship practical meaning: Drug Inspector experience supplies the inspection and enforcement foundation, while ADC(I) adds supervisory, coordination, and broader regulatory-accountability responsibilities. Actual movement, vacancies, seniority, training, and eligibility are governed by the applicable cadre rules and recruitment orders.
bullets: Drug Inspector → Assistant Drugs Controller (India): official Medical Device Vertical example requires eight years' regular service and training.
Assistant Drugs Controller (India) → Deputy Drugs Controller (India): the 2025 CDSCO rule specifies five years' regular service in Level 11 and prescribed training.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: For the central Medical Device Vertical, the 2024 recruitment rules specify Pay Level 11, ₹67,700–₹2,08,700, for ADC(I). This is the pay-matrix level and range stated in those rules, rather than a promise of take-home salary. Actual remuneration at any point depends on the applicable pay rules, dearness allowance, house-rent or transport allowances, location, deductions, and the employee's pay-stage placement.

Compensation for similarly titled state-government posts can differ because state cadres use their own recruitment rules and pay structures. Applicants should verify the salary, allowances, vacancy count, selection method, age limits, and application dates in the live official advertisement for the particular employer and cadre.
bullets: Use Pay Level 11 only as a dated central Medical Device Vertical reference.
Confirm current total pay and conditions from the issuing authority's recruitment notice and applicable pay orders.
